LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BARRED BECAUSE THEY ARE NOT BASED ON STATUTE. GOV. CODE 815. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALSO SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE IT IS PREEMPTED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE CALIFORNIA WORKERS COMPENSATION ACT. THE COURT GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. FURTHER, THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST TO CHANGE THE FACE PAGE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO ACCURATELY REFLECT THE CAUSES OF ACTION CONTAINED WITHIN.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Lawrence Hecimovich of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3933) for defendant and moving party; Shyamala T. Rajender of the Rajender Law Offices (227.4487) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: Defendant's demurrer to the 1st and 4th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred because they are not based on statute. Gov. Code 815. Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action is also sustained without leave to amend because it is preempted by the exclusive remedy provisions of the California Workers Compensation Act. The court granted leave to amend as to the 2nd cause of action. Order signed in open court. Further, the court granted plaintiff's oral request to change the face page of the first amended complaint to accurately reflect the causes of action contained within. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To Complaint (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Recusal Of Judge Busch)
Off Calendar Jun-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 1st cause of action. Sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489
